metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: [Food](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/food/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: [Rob Morren](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/rob-morren/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: [Jelmer Schreurs](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/jelmer-schreurs/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: The Dutch food sector is having a good year. ABN AMRO expects volume growth in 2025 to exceed the previously issued forecast of 1%. A great development, behind which lies an unruly reality with increasingly erratic chains, rising costs and high demands from customers. 'Entrepreneurs who can deliver and are agile will make the difference.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: 'In the food industry, security of supply and reliability are more important than ever. Companies that have a good map of their chains, use data intelligently and maintain long-term relationships with suppliers and customers, are stronger in these turbulent times.' This is what Melanie Murk, Agri & Food sector economist at ABN AMRO, says.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: The food industry is a pillar of the Dutch economy. With a production value of €114.4 billion in 2023, the sector accounts for almost a fifth of total industrial production. In addition, according to the Federation of the Dutch Food Industry (FNLI), the sector created 163,000 direct jobs and 398,000 indirect jobs, or 4.9% of total employment in the Netherlands.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Sales are firmly internationally oriented: 39% of turnover goes to the domestic market (€ 44.1 billion), while 61% (€ 70.3 billion) is exported. The international character also makes the sector vulnerable, Murk emphasizes: 'A large majority of companies in the food industry are concerned about international geopolitics - such as wars and trade tensions - and about changes in demand. These concerns are not entirely unfounded.'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: > “The extreme price increases are behind us, but entrepreneurs must take volatility in chains and raw material prices into account.”

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: It is striking that the forecasts for the sector are being adjusted. While ABN AMRO previously expected volume growth of 1% in 2025, higher growth is now expected in 2025. Murk sees it as a sign of resilience. 'The extreme price increases are behind us. We see that domestic demand is picking up and that exports are also growing this year. But entrepreneurs must take volatility in chains and raw material prices into account.' The forecast for 2026 is not yet final, but based on current developments in the market and recent economic expectations from ABN AMRO, in which both consumption and exports increase slightly and inflation decreases, slight growth is also expected for 2026.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Despite the more favorable growth forecast, there are also significant challenges ahead. 'The sector is faced with structurally higher costs and a tight labor market. In addition, the sector strives to further make the company and the supply chain more sustainable, not least due to increasingly strict requirements from customers and governments. That puts pressure on margins, but also offers an opportunity to distinguish yourself, says Murk.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Climate change also has a direct impact. For example, last year floods in Spain caused many citrus harvests to fail, and extremes elsewhere in the world led to higher prices, especially for coffee and cocoa. Murk: 'These types of incidents are becoming more and more common, which makes it even more urgent to organize security of supply.'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Moreover, due to rising demand, delivery to European customers is no longer always self-evident: demand in South America, Asia and Africa is increasing due to a growing population and increasing prosperity.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: ## Hillfresh: chain security through focus and relationships

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Hillfresh is a medium-sized family business with a turnover of approximately €150 million. From Barendrecht it mainly supplies citrus fruits, grapes and melons to customers in the Netherlands, Germany, Poland and other European countries. 'We consciously choose to specialize. You have to make it clear what you are really good at and what you deliver added value,” says director-owner Hans van den Heuvel. This choice makes the company more agile in a dynamic market.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: 'Due to the growing world population and an emerging middle class on other continents, the international trade in fresh fruit and vegetables has changed from push to pull and we are no longer automatically at the forefront in Europe. In the past, growers and traders mainly wanted to be able to sell their crops; Now it's all about that you, as a buyer, have access to the right sources. This requires a strong network of growers and long-term relationships," says Van den Heuvel.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: In line with the above lies the other major threat: geopolitics. This makes supply chains vulnerable and can cause sales markets to disappear overnight. Recent examples include the trade sanctions against Russia and the Trump administration's tariffs. This hits some companies in the food sector hard. The Dutch food sector exports €2.3 billion worth of products to the United States every year, approximately 3% of the sector total. This mainly concerns alcoholic drinks, fish, crustaceans and molluscs, vegetables, fruit, cheese and chocolate. According to ABN AMRO, the new import duties could reduce Dutch food exports to the US by 23%. “On the total of the food sector, 23% of 3% is not much,” Murk qualifies. 'But you will only just be in that part of the market. Such a levy will be harsh for companies that rely heavily on exports to the US and that cannot easily move to other sales areas.'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: > “The time of pure focus on efficiency and just-in-time is over. Companies are building up more inventories again.”

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: According to Murk, the success of companies increasingly revolves around supply chain security. This means: organizing your chain in such a way that you can always deliver as best as possible. At the right time, at the right price and in the right quantity. “Companies that really understand their customers, that think about product range and timing, and that are at the same time predictable and transparent in deliveries, win market share,” Murk sums up. Such an approach requires very close contact with suppliers and customers, so that you always have your finger on the pulse. In addition, it requires spreading risks by diversifying suppliers and sales markets. 'The time of pure focus on efficiency and just-in-time is over. Companies are building up more inventories again and working with multiple suppliers. That is a dilemma, because it is at the expense of margins and profit, but at the same time it is necessary to guarantee continuity.'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: > “We ensure that exactly what was agreed in terms of volume, quality and time is delivered. That is our core and our new positioning: we are the peak of fresh produce.”

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Murk sees room for growth and additional returns mainly in smart innovations and an even better response to the rapidly changing wishes of consumers. “A growing divide is visible: on the one hand a focus on health and sustainability, on the other hand the need for convenience and enjoyment,” she outlines. She sees plenty of examples: 'Producers market hybrid hamburgers with half meat and half vegetables or seaweed, which still taste like meat. There are more and more so-called functional foods, such as protein bars, sports cookies and enriched water.' Moreover, healthy food must be tasty and easy to prepare, she notes. 'Families and young consumers are sensitive to this. At the same time, there remains room for pampering moments: matcha tea, pistachio ice cream in Dubai style, mango & pumpkin sandwich fillings and other new flavor combinations find their way to the shelves. Eating is not just about nutrition, but also about enjoyment and sharing.'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: ## The customer wants the right volume, of the right quality, on time

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: With a view to maximum security of supply, Hillfresh opted years ago to spread risk across different continents, countries and suppliers, so that shocks due to climate or geopolitics can be better absorbed. Everything in the operation also revolves around reliability. 'The customer doesn't want a headache. We ensure that exactly what was agreed in terms of volume, quality and time is delivered. That is our core and our new positioning: we are the peak of fresh produce.'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Innovation mainly comes from collaboration. For example, Hillfresh introduced the exclusive Limelon, a melon with a fresh sweet and sour taste that Hillfresh has grown exclusively in Spain and Italy in recent years and is sold in the Netherlands through Albert Heijn. 'Varieties with distinctive properties give categories new impetus. In collaboration with seed breeders, our role is to bring this innovation to the market.'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Another track of innovation is in the way in which companies produce and collaborate. Due to staff shortages, automation is becoming increasingly necessary. From robotics in production lines to digital twins and AI-driven quality controls, the sector is investing in technology to maintain productivity.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Data sharing in the chain also plays a growing role. Suppliers are increasingly recording more information about production methods and quality. AI helps to control and structure these information flows. 'Smart use of data can reduce waste and errors in the chain. This makes processes faster and more reliable and saves raw materials,” says Murk.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: Food waste also remains a point of attention on the sidelines of the industry, with room for optimization. Among Dutch consumers, waste fell by 23% between 2015 and 2022, from 43.3 to 33.4 kilos per person per year. If you include the waste in the catering industry and industry, this still amounts to 127 kilos per capita per year.

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: In this context, companies that think ahead are particularly successful. They distinguish themselves through flexibility and cooperation. Murk: 'Companies that invest in digitalization, that organize their processes flexibly and that foster long-term relationships with suppliers and customers, have an advantage. They can respond more quickly to shocks and seize opportunities.' She also believes it is important to look beyond volume. Growth is increasingly reflected in value. Not how many tons are sold, but which tons. Murk: 'It's about the return: which products yield the most and how do you ensure that you get them to the customer on time and reliably?'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: What will the sector look like in five years? Murk expects a further shift to vegetable proteins and less processed food in the Netherlands. The influence of young generations will also increase. 'They look at food differently. They want to know what they eat, how it is produced and are influenced by social media. That changes the playing field.' However, the core remains the same, she predicts: security of supply, innovation and cooperation determine who survives. 'Food remains, we continue to eat. So whoever can deliver what the consumer wants, in a predictable way and at an acceptable price, holds the key to growth.'

metadata.source_title: Growth and major challenges in the food sector

text: From AI and hybrid products to the power of social media: the food sector is changing rapidly. In this video, business banker Maurits Born explains where your growth opportunities lie in 2026.

text: The Dutch food sector is a leader in efficiency and exports. Robotization, digitalization and sustainability are important themes on which entrepreneurs distinguish themselves from many international competitors.
